B1 Friends / Sitcom / Casual Talk ~90s · 今天 ←

The Coffee Run

100Ls Shadowing Material · Lesson 05  ·  ← 上一课
🎧 Main Audio
0:00 / 0:00
今天听了 0 遍
💬 Dialog Script
Rachel
Hey, you're here early! Did something happen?
Chandler
My presentation got pushed to Friday. I've been up since five.
Rachel
Oh no. Coffee first, panic later. Can I get two large lattes?
Chandler
You're a lifesaver. So, how's the boutique going?
Rachel
Really good, actually. We got a new shipment yesterday. The dresses are gorgeous.
Chandler
Nice. Hey, are you coming to Monica's dinner on Thursday?
Rachel
Wouldn't miss it. She texted me about the menu — three courses.
Chandler
Three courses on a Wednesday. Only Monica.
Rachel
She's already stressed about the timing.
Chandler
Of course she is. I live with her. I hear the spreadsheets.
Rachel
The spreadsheets?
Chandler
She has a dinner timeline in Excel. Color-coded.
Rachel
That's terrifying. And also kind of impressive.
Chandler
That's married life, I guess.
Rachel
Here's your latte. Panic after coffee.
Chandler
This might be the best advice you've ever given me.
Rachel：嘿，你来这么早！发生什么事了吗？
Chandler：我的汇报被推到周五了。我五点就起来了。
Rachel：哦不。先喝咖啡，恐慌之后再说。能给我们来两杯大杯拿铁吗？
Chandler：你真是救命恩人。那你精品店最近怎么样？
Rachel：真的很好。昨天到了一批新货。那些裙子特别漂亮。
Chandler：不错。嘿，你周四来Monica的晚餐吗？
Rachel：肯定去。她给我发信息说菜单——三道菜呢。
Chandler：周三吃三道菜。只有Monica会这样。
Rachel：她已经在为时间安排焦虑了。
Chandler：那当然。我和她住在一起。我都能听到那些电子表格的声音。
Rachel：电子表格？
Chandler：她在Excel里做了一份晚餐时间线。还是彩色标注的。
Rachel：太可怕了。但同时又有点让人佩服。
Chandler：我猜这就是婚后生活吧。
Rachel：你的拿铁来了。喝完咖啡再恐慌。
Chandler：这可能是你给过我最好的建议了。
🔁 Sentence Practice
Rachel
Hey, you're here early! Did something happen?
嘿，你来这么早！发生什么事了吗？
Chandler
My presentation got pushed to Friday. I've been up since five.
我的汇报被推到周五了。我五点就起来了。
Rachel
Oh no. Coffee first, panic later. Can I get two large lattes?
哦不。先喝咖啡，恐慌之后再说。能给我们来两杯大杯拿铁吗？
Chandler
You're a lifesaver. So, how's the boutique going?
你真是救命恩人。那你精品店最近怎么样？
Rachel
Really good, actually. We got a new shipment yesterday. The dresses are gorgeous.
真的很好。昨天到了一批新货。那些裙子特别漂亮。
Chandler
Nice. Hey, are you coming to Monica's dinner on Thursday?
不错。嘿，你周四来Monica的晚餐吗？
Rachel
Wouldn't miss it. She texted me about the menu — three courses.
肯定去。她给我发信息说菜单——三道菜呢。
Chandler
Three courses on a Wednesday. Only Monica.
周三吃三道菜。只有Monica会这样。
Rachel
She's already stressed about the timing.
她已经在为时间安排焦虑了。
Chandler
Of course she is. I live with her. I hear the spreadsheets.
那当然。我和她住在一起。我都能听到那些电子表格的声音。
Rachel
The spreadsheets?
电子表格？
Chandler
She has a dinner timeline in Excel. Color-coded.
她在Excel里做了一份晚餐时间线。还是彩色标注的。
Rachel
That's terrifying. And also kind of impressive.
太可怕了。但同时又有点让人佩服。
Chandler
That's married life, I guess.
我猜这就是婚后生活吧。
Rachel
Here's your latte. Panic after coffee.
你的拿铁来了。喝完咖啡再恐慌。
Chandler
This might be the best advice you've ever given me.
这可能是你给过我最好的建议了。
📖 Vocabulary
lifesaver /ˈlaɪfˌseɪvər/ n.
救命恩人；救星
Collocations
be a lifesaverabsolute lifesavertotal lifesaver
Example
That coffee was a real lifesaver this morning.
今早那杯咖啡真的救了我。
Similar
godsendherosavior
shipment /ˈʃɪpmənt/ n.
一批货；货运
Collocations
new shipmentreceive a shipmentshipment of goods
Example
We're expecting a shipment of new inventory next week.
我们下周预计收到一批新库存。
Similar
deliveryconsignmentbatch
color-coded /ˈkʌlər ˌkoʊdɪd/ adj.
用颜色标注的；彩色编码的
Collocations
color-coded chartcolor-coded systemcolor-coded calendar
Example
The schedule was color-coded by department.
日程表按部门用颜色标注。
Similar
organizedlabeledcategorized
push (sth) to /pʊʃ/ phr.v.
将……推迟到（另一时间）
Collocations
push the meeting to Mondaypush backpush the deadline
Example
The launch was pushed to next month due to technical issues.
由于技术问题，发布被推迟到下个月。
Similar
postponereschedulemove back
wouldn't miss it /ˈwʊdnt mɪs ɪt/ phr.
绝对不会错过；肯定会去
Collocations
I wouldn't miss it for the worldwouldn't miss this
Example
A free concert in the park? I wouldn't miss it!
公园里有免费演唱会？我肯定不会错过！
Similar
definitely goingcount me inI'll be there
🔗 Connected Speech
"you're here early"
→ yer here early
linking /r/
you're ends with /r/, which links into here. Sounds like one smooth phrase: yer-here-early.
"Coffee first, panic later"
→ Coffee firs(t), panic later
t-reduction
The final /t/ in first is often dropped before a consonant in natural speech. firs' flows right into panic.
"kind of impressive"
→ kinda impressive
reduction
kind of reduces to kinda in casual speech. Common in informal American English.
"I've been up since five"
→ I'v been up since five
contraction + weak form
I've contracts to /aɪv/. been reduces to /bɪn/. The phrase flows as a single rhythmic unit.
